summary: Challenged prior QA turn (turn_687bfd76c850ef17) which did not touch launch artifacts; updated all four launch_ready gate artifacts (MESSAGING.md, LAUNCH_PLAN.md, CONTENT_CALENDAR.md, ANNOUNCEMENT.md) to cover M25 static PII field-name redaction hints with Constraint 15/16 framing; npm test still passes with 8 scenarios on dirty workspace.

decisions.statement: Challenged the prior accepted turn (turn_687bfd76c850ef17, role=qa, phase=qa, HEAD 7abce74) rather than rubber-stamping its launch state. That turn ran in the qa phase and updated qa-owned artifacts (acceptance-matrix.md, RELEASE_NOTES.md, ROADMAP.md, ship-verdict.md, website/docs/manifest-format.md) for M25; it did not modify any launch_ready gate artifact. Independent on-disk audit of MESSAGING.md, LAUNCH_PLAN.md, CONTENT_CALENDAR.md, and ANNOUNCEMENT.md on HEAD 7abce74 found zero references to M25, pii_fields, PII_CANONICAL_NAMES, or extractPiiFieldHints before this turn — even though M25 shipped end-to-end (REQ-088–REQ-093 accepted PASS, qa_ship_verdict gate passed). Independently re-ran `npm test` on HEAD 7abce74: exits 0 with 'Smoke tests passed' and 'Eval regression harness passed (8 scenarios)' — confirming the pii-field-hint-extraction-determinism eval scenario is live.
decisions.rationale: Workflow contract requires the launch role to challenge the prior turn explicitly and to ship M25-aware launch copy that an external reader can defend against the SYSTEM_SPEC Constraint 15/16 framing boundaries. Skipping the challenge would have left the four launch artifacts silently lagging the shipped product surface — the exact failure mode that DEC-565, DEC-586, and prior product_marketing turns flagged for earlier milestones.

decisions.statement: Updated .planning/MESSAGING.md to add: (a) a new Launch Correction bullet naming M25, the auto-populated `redaction.pii_fields`, the `toLowerCase()` + strip `_`/`-` normalization rule, the frozen 36-name `PII_CANONICAL_NAMES` set in `src/cli.js`, the whole-key invariant, the no-`sensitivity_class`-escalation invariant, the no-`log_level`/`mask_in_traces`/`retention_days` auto-population invariant, the digest-flip-requires-re-approval flow, and the Constraint 16 framing boundary; (b) seven new Product Truth lines covering the pure in-memory function (no source-file read, no value inspection, no network I/O, no PII/NLP library import), the M25-is-active-by-default-no-flag invariant, the framework-agnostic scope (Express path-params, Fastify body fields, NestJS-derived capabilities all flow through the same function), the explicit 36-name 9-category canonical set, the whole-key matching examples (`user_email` matches, `email_template_id` does not), the no-auto-escalation rule, and the M13 capability-digest-flip-requires-re-approval flow; (c) a Claims We Can Defend bullet for the static, name-only PII field-name hint extraction; and (d) three new Claims We Must Not Make bullets forbidding PII-detection/GDPR/HIPAA/PCI/automated-redaction framing (Constraint 16), forbidding any implication that M25 adds a new command/flag/env-var gate or auto-escalates `sensitivity_class`/auto-populates other redaction fields, and forbidding any implication that matching is substring/tail/regex/value-aware or that the canonical set extends beyond the published frozen V1.6 36 names.
decisions.rationale: MESSAGING.md is the source of truth for boundary-setting copy that downstream channels (LinkedIn, blog, X, community) inherit. Without an M25-specific Launch Correction bullet plus matching Product Truth lines, downstream copy could either omit M25 entirely or — worse — reach for the easier 'PII detection' framing that Constraint 16 explicitly forbids. The Claims We Must Not Make bullets are the strongest available guardrail against that overclaim.

decisions.statement: Updated .planning/LAUNCH_PLAN.md to add: (a) a new Challenge To Prior Framing bullet naming M25 and the new auto-populated `redaction.pii_fields` shape, the whole-key normalization rule, the no-`sensitivity_class`-escalation rule, the digest-flip-requires-re-approval flow, the Constraint 16 framing boundary, and the frozen V1.6 list invariant; (b) a Must-publish-at-launch line update inserting the M25 static, name-only PII field-name redaction hints into the verified product boundary enumeration alongside the existing M24 Fastify body extraction and the rest of the scan/manifest/approve/compile/serve/policy chain; and (c) an Expectation-mismatch-on-MCP risk update clarifying that `redaction.pii_fields` is a verify-time name-match statement, not a runtime PII-handling guarantee, that copy must not describe M25 as 'PII detection,' 'PII validation,' 'GDPR/HIPAA/PCI-compliant,' or 'automated redaction,' that copy must not imply M25 auto-escalates `sensitivity_class` or auto-populates other redaction fields, that copy must not imply matching is substring or tail-aware (`email_template_id` does NOT match `email`), and that copy must not announce or hint at PII names outside the published, frozen V1.6 canonical 36-name set.
decisions.rationale: LAUNCH_PLAN.md scopes what we publish at launch and what risks we own. Without the M25 entry on the must-publish boundary, the launch could ship copy that names M24 but silently omits M25 — the same drift mode that previously forced re-runs for M16/M18/M19/M20/M21/M22/M23/M24. The Expectation-mismatch-on-MCP risk update adds explicit guardrails against the most likely overclaim path.

decisions.statement: Updated .planning/CONTENT_CALENDAR.md to add: (a) a launch-day callout appended to the terminal-first post noting that `tusq manifest` now auto-populates `capability.redaction.pii_fields` by matching `input_schema.properties` keys whole-key against the frozen 36-name V1.6 canonical PII set after `toLowerCase()` + strip `_`/`-` normalization, in source-declaration order with original casing preserved, no new flag, framework-agnostic, no `sensitivity_class` escalation, no `log_level`/`mask_in_traces`/`retention_days` auto-population, byte-identical for capabilities without canonical PII keys, and explicitly framed as 'well-known PII field-name hints' — not 'PII detection' or 'GDPR/HIPAA/PCI-compliant'; and (b) a Day 3-4 'PII field-name hints now appear in `redaction.pii_fields`' post focused on M25 covering the matching algorithm, the 9-category canonical set, the no-new-flag invariant, the framework-agnostic scope, the whole-key examples, the no-`sensitivity_class`-escalation/no-`log_level`-`mask_in_traces`-`retention_days` invariant, the byte-identical fall-back, the digest-flip-requires-re-approval flow, the frozen-V1.6-list rule, and the Constraint 16 framing boundary.
decisions.rationale: CONTENT_CALENDAR.md is the channel plan. Without an M25-specific terminal-first callout and a Day 3-4 deep-dive post, M25 would ship to GitHub release notes but never to the social/community surface where most early adopters first encounter the product.

decisions.statement: Updated .planning/ANNOUNCEMENT.md to add: (a) a new step 14 in the product-summary workflow for M25 (V1.6) covering the pure in-memory function (no source-file read, no value inspection, no source-text regex, no network I/O, no PII/NLP library import), the explicit 36-name 9-category canonical PII set, the `toLowerCase()` + strip `_`/`-` normalization rule, the source-declaration-order-with-original-casing-preserved output rule, the M25-is-active-by-default-no-flag invariant, the framework-agnostic scope, the whole-key matching examples, the no-`sensitivity_class`-escalation invariant, the no-`log_level`/`mask_in_traces`/`retention_days` auto-population invariant, the byte-identical fall-back invariant, the digest-flip-requires-re-approval flow, the frozen-V1.6-list invariant, and the Constraint 16 framing boundary; (b) an updated proof-points paragraph naming the static, name-only PII field-name hint extraction with the same set of invariants; (c) a bash-block comment block inserted after `tusq manifest` explaining what M25 does, what whole-key matches versus does not match, what M25 does not auto-escalate or auto-populate, what byte-identical means for non-PII capabilities, and what the digest-flip behavior implies for `tusq diff` and `tools/list`; (d) an updated How To Try It Today bullet for `tusq.manifest.json` review that walks through inspecting `redaction.pii_fields` on every capability with explicit Constraint 16 framing and explicit reviewer-still-owns-`log_level`/`mask_in_traces`/`retention_days` framing; (e) an X-post update naming the M25 extraction with the matching algorithm and the not-PII-detection callout; and (f) a community-post V1 scope bullet beneath the existing M24 Fastify-extraction bullet for the M25 static, name-only PII field-name hint extraction with the not-validator-backed/not-runtime-enforced framing.
decisions.rationale: ANNOUNCEMENT.md is the highest-leverage launch surface — it feeds the GitHub release post, the blog draft, the X thread, the LinkedIn post, and the community post. The M25 entry has to land in five places (workflow step, proof points, bash demo, how-to-try-it, and the explicit V1-scope enumeration) so that no downstream copy path leaks the older 'edit `pii_fields` by hand' framing or the easier-to-reach-for 'PII detection' overclaim.

decisions.statement: Did NOT modify reserved orchestrator state files (.agentxchain/state.json, history.jsonl, decision-ledger.jsonl, lock.json). Did NOT modify PM-owned planning artifacts (PM_SIGNOFF.md, ROADMAP.md, SYSTEM_SPEC.md, command-surface.md) because launch phase is not the right time to mutate planning-gate artifacts and none of them have M25 drift (planning_signoff for M25 already passed under DEC-602/turn_b720108c0495ed22). Did NOT modify dev-owned src/ or QA-owned acceptance-matrix.md/ship-verdict.md/RELEASE_NOTES.md/website/docs/manifest-format.md — M25 implementation, acceptance criteria, release notes, and developer documentation already carry the shipped-state framing on HEAD 7abce74 and were the source I referenced for canonical M25 claims (especially RELEASE_NOTES.md lines 335-368 and SYSTEM_SPEC.md lines 2280-2407). Only the four launch_ready gate artifacts were modified this turn.
decisions.rationale: Write-authority for the launch role is scoped to the launch_ready gate artifacts. Mutating dev/QA/PM artifacts at this stage would either re-open already-passed gates or introduce drift between roles that have already signed off on M25. The on-disk M25 truth is canonical in src/cli.js, RELEASE_NOTES.md, and SYSTEM_SPEC.md; my job is to bring the launch surface into alignment with that truth, not to restate or modify it.

decisions.statement: All four launch_ready gate artifacts now cover M25 on HEAD 7abce74 (workspace dirty): MESSAGING.md 9 M25-surface mentions, LAUNCH_PLAN.md 3, CONTENT_CALENDAR.md 2, ANNOUNCEMENT.md 9 (23 total). Upstream gates (planning_signoff for M25 passed under DEC-602, implementation_complete for M25 passed under DEC-603–DEC-608, qa_ship_verdict for M25 passed under DEC-609–DEC-613) are all passed. `npm test` exits 0 with 'Smoke tests passed' and 'Eval regression harness passed (8 scenarios)' — confirming the pii-field-hint-extraction-determinism scenario is present and all M25 smoke items pass on the modified workspace. The 12-command surface remains enumerated correctly in every artifact; M25 added no new command or flag (it is a silent `tusq manifest` extraction upgrade), so no command-count copy changed.
decisions.rationale: Independent verification per protocol rule 2: I ran `npm test` and `node bin/tusq.js help` myself and counted M25 mentions across the four artifacts via Grep, rather than relying on the prior turn's claims. The 23-total-mention count is the smallest defensible footprint that still covers the Launch Correction, Product Truth, Defendable Claims, Forbidden Claims, Challenge To Prior Framing, Must-publish boundary, Expectation-mismatch risk, launch-day post, deep-dive post, workflow step, proof points, bash demo, how-to-try, X post, and community post requirements.

decisions.statement: Status is needs_human because the launch_ready exit gate explicitly requires human approval before run completion; an agent product_marketing role cannot self-declare gate satisfaction regardless of artifact completeness. proposed_next_role is 'human' so the orchestrator routes to the reviewer. run_completion_request is true because the launch phase is the final phase and all four gate artifacts now accurately describe the shipped M25 boundary. phase_transition_request is null because launch is terminal.
decisions.rationale: Mirrors the same protocol stance taken under DEC-571, DEC-583, DEC-593, and DEC-602 — agent roles cannot self-cross human-gated boundaries even when their own work is complete. The reviewer needs to confirm that the launch artifacts actually describe what shipped before run_completion is honored.

summary: Derived M27 as a bounded reviewer-facing PII redaction review subcommand (tusq redaction review) that aggregates M25/M26 PII evidence with frozen advisory text and strictly does not mutate the manifest, retention_days, log_level, mask_in_traces, sensitivity_class, serve, or policy verify.

decisions.statement: M27 scope is exactly one new CLI noun `redaction` with one subcommand `review`: a read-only reviewer-facing report that aggregates every capability's M25 redaction.pii_fields and M26 redaction.pii_categories alongside a frozen per-category advisory line from a new PII_REVIEW_ADVISORY_BY_CATEGORY lookup. No manifest mutation, no new dependency, no runtime enforcement, no change to any existing command's observable behavior.
decisions.rationale: The charter from intake intent intent_1776860902785_6360 asked for the next bounded redaction/retention-defaults step as reviewer-owned policy recommendations or review aids derived from existing pii_categories, without automatically mutating retention_days, log_level, mask_in_traces, sensitivity_class, policy verify, or serve behavior. A reviewer-facing read-only report is the smallest increment that surfaces the M25/M26 evidence per capability with frozen per-category advisory text while preserving every existing invariant. Rejected three tempting alternatives: auto-populating retention defaults per category (violates Constraint 18; scanner cannot choose retention responsibly), a write-capable `redaction apply-defaults` command (conflates reviewer judgment with static analysis), and extending `tusq review` with a `--pii` flag (conflates approval-gate review with PII-aware review).

decisions.statement: M27 adds a new CLI noun `redaction` parallel to the existing `policy` noun (M21/M22/M23), not a top-level `pii-review` command or a flag on the existing `tusq review` approval-gate command.
decisions.rationale: `tusq review` already means the M15 approval-gate command; grafting PII review onto it would conflate two concerns and risk breaking CI callers. A separate noun `redaction` with one subcommand `review` mirrors the shape already established by `tusq policy` + `init`/`verify`, keeps M27 observationally invisible to every existing caller that doesn't explicitly invoke it, and leaves room for future redaction-related subcommands without polluting any existing surface. `tusq redaction` (no subcommand) prints an enumerate-subcommands block identical in shape to `tusq policy`.

decisions.statement: The V1.8 nine-entry PII_REVIEW_ADVISORY_BY_CATEGORY advisory set is frozen in src/cli.js and enumerated in SYSTEM_SPEC § M27. No override file, no environment variable, no flag, no locale in V1.8. Every advisory ends with a reviewer-directive reminder ('reviewer: ...') so the output makes clear the decision still belongs to the reviewer.
decisions.rationale: Allowing overrides would create a reviewer-bypass failure mode (a team could edit an advisory to 'no further action needed' and silently skip review) and would cause unpredictable CI diffs across environments. The governance argument for freezing PII_CANONICAL_NAMES (Constraint 15) and PII_CATEGORY_BY_NAME (Constraint 17) applies verbatim to the advisory set: any addition OR wording change is a material governance event that MUST land under its own ROADMAP milestone with fresh re-approval and a RELEASE_NOTES entry. Multi-locale advisory sets are reserved for V2 under a plugin interface.

decisions.statement: M27 causes zero capability_digest flips on first post-M27 run because the manifest is not mutated. In contrast with M25 (narrow flip on PII-name capabilities) and M26 (broad flip across every capability), M27 is observationally invisible in `tusq diff`.
decisions.rationale: Constraint 20 codifies M27 as strictly read-only: it MUST NOT mutate tusq.manifest.json, MUST NOT write to .tusq/execution-policy.json, MUST NOT change the observable behavior of scan/manifest/compile/serve/approve/diff/docs/version/policy init/policy verify (default or --strict). The only observable side effects are stdout, stderr, and a process exit code. A smoke test asserts the manifest's mtime and content are unchanged after any invocation, and that policy verify (default and --strict) produces byte-identical stdout and exit code before and after running M27 on the same fixture.

decisions.statement: Challenged the prior accepted turn (turn_e21bcbf7e08e9f4c, role=product_marketing, phase=launch, HEAD c1cd53a) rather than rubber-stamping. The M26 launch pass completed (DEC-887–DEC-889) but cannot satisfy a fresh M27 planning acceptance contract; M27 requires its own planning artifacts.
decisions.rationale: Independent verification of baseline HEAD c1cd53a on 2026-04-22: `npm test` exits 0 with 'Smoke tests passed' and 'Eval regression harness passed (9 scenarios)', confirming the M26 pii-category-label-determinism scenario ships alongside the M25, M24, M23, and prior scenarios. The M26 ROADMAP items are already marked checked in the accepted baseline; no ROADMAP check-boxes needed to be re-flipped this turn. Marked zero M27 items checked — M27 is implementation-ready planned work, not shipped work; dev owns shipping, QA owns REQ-101+ verification.

decisions.statement: Modified only the four PM-owned planning_signoff gate artifacts: .planning/PM_SIGNOFF.md, .planning/ROADMAP.md, .planning/SYSTEM_SPEC.md, .planning/command-surface.md. Did NOT modify reserved orchestrator state files, QA-owned artifacts, launch-owned artifacts, dev-owned src/, or intake-template stubs.
decisions.rationale: Reserved state files (.agentxchain/state.json, history.jsonl, decision-ledger.jsonl, lock.json) are orchestrator-owned. QA-owned acceptance-matrix.md/ship-verdict.md/RELEASE_NOTES.md and launch-owned MESSAGING.md/LAUNCH_PLAN.md/CONTENT_CALENDAR.md/ANNOUNCEMENT.md belong to later phases and have no M27 drift to fix. Intake-template stubs (distribution-checklist.md, platform-support.md) are not PM-owned gate artifacts. src/ belongs to the dev role. Writing only the four PM-owned artifacts keeps the turn narrowly scoped to the planning gate.

objections.statement: The M27 advisory wording for `email`, `phone`, and `address` shares the same 'contact-data policy' framing, which is accurate but means three of the nine categories carry near-identical sentences. A future release may want to differentiate them (e.g., distinct retention windows for email vs physical address under some jurisdictions); the frozen-list governance rule will force that as a material milestone rather than a silent edit, which is the desired outcome but worth flagging so dev/QA don't normalize the wording toward each other during implementation.

verification.evidence_summary: Baseline HEAD c1cd53a confirmed clean before planning edits; npm test exits 0 with 'Smoke tests passed' and 'Eval regression harness passed (9 scenarios)' both before and after the four PM-owned planning artifact edits, confirming that .planning-only changes do not affect the shipped product behavior. Verified M27 references now exist in ROADMAP.md (section + 4 new risk rows), SYSTEM_SPEC.md (new § M27 + Constraints 19 and 20, constraint ordering 15→16→17→18→19→20 restored), command-surface.md (new § M27 Product CLI Surface), and PM_SIGNOFF.md (new preamble paragraph + Challenges 38, 39, 40).

summary: Tightened the accepted M27 plan by closing three implementation-ambiguity gaps (empty-capabilities manifest behavior, stdout/stderr discipline on error paths, optional top-level manifest field fallback + advisory byte-exactness) across all four PM-owned planning artifacts without re-scoping M27 or adding new dependencies.

decisions.statement: This PM turn does not re-scope M27; it closes three implementation-ambiguity gaps in the accepted M27 plan on HEAD 381feb7 so two defensible implementations cannot ship and diverge on reviewer-visible behavior. No new command, no new flag, no new dependency, no new constraint; Constraints 19 and 20 remain unchanged. ROADMAP § M27 items grow from 17 to 21, SYSTEM_SPEC § M27 gains three subsections, command-surface.md § M27 gains one Failure UX row, one Stream discipline callout, and one Local-Only Invariant row.
decisions.rationale: Independent audit of the four accepted M27 artifacts found three legitimate spec gaps: (a) `capabilities: []` behavior unspecified — could ship as exit 0 or exit 1; (b) stderr-only discipline implied but not asserted — an implementation could write `{error: ...}` to stdout in --json mode and pass the prior spec; (c) missing `manifest_version`/`generated_at` fallback unspecified and advisory byte-exactness (em-dash U+2014 vs ASCII hyphen U+002D) unstated. Closing these before dev picks up M27 prevents implementation drift and preserves the reviewer-pipe-to-file UX that the plan already promises.

decisions.statement: Empty-capabilities manifest (`capabilities: []`) MUST exit 0 with `No capabilities in manifest — nothing to review.` in human mode and `{manifest_path, manifest_version, generated_at, capabilities: []}` in --json mode; this is distinct from missing/non-array `capabilities` which stays exit 1 with `Invalid manifest: missing capabilities array`. stdout MUST be empty on every exit-1 path (asserted by smoke). Missing `manifest_version`/`generated_at` emit `null` in --json / `unknown` in human output and MUST NOT be re-stamped with wall-clock time. Frozen advisory strings use em-dash U+2014 exactly; ASCII hyphen U+002D or en-dash U+2013 drift is a material governance event.
decisions.rationale: These four concrete rules eliminate the divergence risk: scaffold-friendly empty-array handling, zero-byte-stdout on pipe-to-file failure, content-determinism preservation for older fixtures, and byte-exact advisory wording so CI snapshots stay stable across releases. Each is testable via smoke fixture and each maps to one or more ROADMAP items, a SYSTEM_SPEC § M27 subsection, and a command-surface.md row.

decisions.statement: Challenged the prior accepted turn (turn_4a4545d1e454f260, role=pm, phase=planning, HEAD 381feb7) by auditing its four artifacts rather than rubber-stamping; the prior turn defined M27 cleanly at high level (one CLI noun, read-only, zero new dependencies) but left three implementation details under-specified that would have surfaced as dev-role friction. This turn is a tightening pass within the same planning phase, not a re-scope.
decisions.rationale: Parent run (run_da9aca11c8070842) completed planning + some implementation before stalling at turn:failed_start for dev; the prior PM planning artifacts are the right starting baseline, but a fresh PM audit is exactly the right gate to close gaps before a new dev turn picks M27 back up. Rubber-stamping would have shipped the divergence-risk inherited from the parent.

decisions.statement: Modified only the four PM-owned planning_signoff gate artifacts: .planning/PM_SIGNOFF.md, .planning/ROADMAP.md, .planning/SYSTEM_SPEC.md, .planning/command-surface.md. Did NOT modify reserved orchestrator state files (.agentxchain/state.json, history.jsonl, decision-ledger.jsonl, lock.json). Did NOT modify dev-owned src/, QA-owned acceptance-matrix.md/ship-verdict.md/RELEASE_NOTES.md, launch-owned MESSAGING.md/LAUNCH_PLAN.md/CONTENT_CALENDAR.md/ANNOUNCEMENT.md, or intake-template stubs (distribution-checklist.md, platform-support.md).
decisions.rationale: Scope hygiene: a PM tightening pass belongs in the four planning artifacts only. QA/launch/dev artifacts describe shipped state on HEAD 381feb7 for prior milestones (M1–M26) and have nothing to say about M27 implementation ambiguity.

decisions.statement: Status is needs_human and proposed_next_role is 'human' because the planning_signoff exit gate for this run explicitly requires human approval before transitioning to the implementation phase. phase_transition_request is null because an agent may not self-transition across a human-gated boundary.
decisions.rationale: Protocol rule: agent PM cannot self-declare gate satisfaction for a gate that requires human approval regardless of artifact completeness. The human reviewer will decide whether the M27 plan (with these tightenings) is implementation-ready.

objections.statement: The empty-capabilities case (exit 0 with `No capabilities in manifest — nothing to review.`) is deliberately scaffold-friendly, but a future lint/CI configuration could reasonably want the opposite behavior (exit non-zero when there's nothing to review so the operator is forced to scan first). V1.8 ships the scaffold-friendly behavior; any future `--require-capabilities` or `--fail-on-empty` flag would be a material V2 addition. Flagging so dev/QA don't interpret 'exit 0 on empty' as an invitation to add a flag now.

verification.evidence_summary: Baseline HEAD 381feb7 confirmed clean before planning edits; `npm test` exits 0 with 'Smoke tests passed' and 'Eval regression harness passed (9 scenarios)' both before and after the four PM-owned planning artifact edits, confirming that .planning-only changes do not affect shipped product behavior. `git diff --stat .planning/` confirms exactly four files changed: PM_SIGNOFF.md +14, ROADMAP.md +6, SYSTEM_SPEC.md +38, command-surface.md +16/-6 lines. Verified M27 tightenings now appear: ROADMAP.md § M27 gains four new bullets (empty-capabilities, stream discipline, optional-field fallback, advisory byte-exactness) plus two new Key Risk rows; SYSTEM_SPEC.md § M27 gains Edge-Case and Empty-Manifest Handling, Optional Top-Level Manifest Field Fallback, and Advisory Byte-Exactness Invariant subsections; command-surface.md § M27 Failure UX gains the empty-capabilities exit-0 row and a Stream discipline callout, Local-Only Invariants gains an Advisory byte-exactness row; PM_SIGNOFF.md gains a new preamble paragraph describing this turn and Challenge 41.

decisions.statement: Fixed M27(b) smoke failure by passing --manifest explicitly in JSON-mode comparison calls, avoiding macOS /var vs /private/var tmpdir symlink mismatch.
decisions.rationale: On macOS, os.tmpdir() returns /var/folders/... but subprocess process.cwd() returns /private/var/folders/... (the real path). The test compared manifest_path from the report (real path) against m27ManifestPath (symlink path). Passing --manifest explicitly makes the CLI use the test-supplied path directly, giving identical strings on both sides.

decisions.statement: Challenged the prior accepted turn (turn_7de1d2affebb3f59, role=qa, phase=qa, HEAD c8ffa38) rather than rubber-stamping its launch state. That turn operated in the qa phase and re-verified ship verdict plus 108 acceptance criteria on HEAD c8ffa38 — it did not modify any launch-owned artifact. Independent on-disk audit of MESSAGING.md, LAUNCH_PLAN.md, CONTENT_CALENDAR.md, and ANNOUNCEMENT.md on HEAD 93c84b3 found ZERO references to M27, `tusq redaction review`, `PII_REVIEW_ADVISORY`, Constraint 19, or Constraint 20 before this turn — even though M27 shipped end-to-end (REQ-094–REQ-108 accepted PASS, qa_ship_verdict gate passed, `node bin/tusq.js redaction review --help` returns the full three-flag surface on HEAD). The launch gate artifacts were stale for M27 exactly the same way they were stale for M25/M26 before DEC-615–DEC-620. Re-ran `npm test` on HEAD 93c84b3: exits 0 with 'Smoke tests passed' and 'Eval regression harness passed (10 scenarios)' — confirms the `redaction-review-determinism` eval scenario is live.
decisions.rationale: Challenging the prior turn is Protocol Rule 1 and prevents silent acceptance of a state where upstream (implementation/QA) has shipped a milestone that launch-owned copy does not yet cover; the same failure mode was fixed for M24/M25/M26 under prior PM launch turns, and M27 drifted exactly the same way.

decisions.statement: Updated .planning/MESSAGING.md to add: (a) a new Launch Correction bullet naming M27 as the V1.8 increment, the new `redaction` top-level CLI noun (13th shipped entry point), the `tusq redaction review [--manifest <path>] [--capability <name>] [--json]` surface, the frozen nine-entry `PII_REVIEW_ADVISORY_BY_CATEGORY` lookup in `src/cli.js`, the strictly-read-only invariant (manifest unchanged, no `.tusq/` write, no `capability_digest` flip), the zero-digest-flip / observationally-invisible-in-`tusq diff` invariant, the `capabilities: []` valid-exit-0-scaffold-state rule distinct from `Invalid manifest: missing capabilities array`, the `manifest_version`/`generated_at` null/unknown copy-verbatim rule with no wall-clock re-stamping, the empty-stdout-on-every-exit-1-path rule with stderr-only error text, the distinct-category-first-appearance-order advisories rule, the em-dash U+2014 byte-exactness invariant, the total-disjoint-frozen-V1.8-advisory-set rule, and the Constraints 19/20 reviewer-aid framing boundary; (b) updated the CLI surface Product Truth line from 12 to 13 top-level entry points including `redaction`; (c) five new Product Truth lines covering `tusq redaction review` flag surface and report shape, the new `redaction` noun as parallel to `policy` (not a flag on `tusq review`), the frozen advisory lookup with em-dash byte-exactness and distinct-category-first-appearance-order rule, the strictly-read-only/no-downstream-command-behavior-change/zero-digest-flip invariants, and the empty-capabilities valid-exit-0-scaffold-state behavior distinct from error paths; (d) a Claims We Can Defend bullet for the read-only reviewer-facing M27 report; and (e) four new Claims We Must Not Make bullets forbidding runtime-PII-enforcement / automated-PII-compliance / automated-redaction-policy / GDPR-HIPAA-PCI-compliance framing, forbidding any implication that M27 mutates the manifest or changes any other command's behavior or re-stamps manifest_version/generated_at, forbidding any implication that `PII_REVIEW_ADVISORY_BY_CATEGORY` strings are editable/overridable/locale-aware/format-configurable/reorderable or that CSV/HTML/Markdown export or `--diff`/`--since`/reviewer-identity-capture modes exist in V1.8, and forbidding any framing of `capabilities: []` as an error or any implication that exit-1 paths write JSON/partial content to stdout.
decisions.rationale: MESSAGING.md is the canonical source for claims used by every downstream launch channel (README, homepage, X, LinkedIn, community post, blog); if the positioning foundation is silent on M27, every downstream asset inherits the silence. All five standard MESSAGING sections (Launch Correction, Product Truth, Claims We Can Defend, Claims We Must Not Make) now carry M27 with the same structural depth the file already uses for M24/M25/M26.

decisions.statement: Updated .planning/LAUNCH_PLAN.md to add: (a) a new Challenge To Prior Framing bullet naming M27 as the V1.8 increment, the `redaction` CLI noun as the 13th shipped entry point parallel to `policy`, the full three-flag surface (`--manifest`, `--capability`, `--json`), the strictly-read-only invariant (manifest unchanged, no `.tusq/` write, no digest flip), the zero-digest-flip / observationally-invisible-in-`tusq diff` invariant, the `capabilities: []` valid-exit-0-scaffold-state rule distinct from the `Invalid manifest: missing capabilities array` error path, the `manifest_version`/`generated_at` null/unknown rule with no wall-clock re-stamping, the empty-stdout-on-every-exit-1-path rule with stderr-only error text, the distinct-category-first-appearance-order advisories rule, the em-dash U+2014 byte-exactness invariant locked by a smoke fixture, the total-disjoint-frozen-V1.8-advisory-set rule, and the Constraints 19/20 reviewer-aid framing boundary; (b) a Must-publish-at-launch line update inserting the M27 read-only reviewer-facing PII redaction review surface into the verified product boundary enumeration alongside the existing M24/M25/M26 surface; and (c) an Expectation-mismatch-on-MCP risk update clarifying that `tusq redaction review` is a reviewer aid rather than runtime enforcement, that copy must not describe M27 as 'runtime PII enforcement,' 'automated PII compliance,' 'automated redaction policy,' or GDPR/HIPAA/PCI-compliant, that copy must not imply M27 mutates the manifest or changes any other command's behavior or auto-approves/auto-escalates anything, that copy must not frame `capabilities: []` as an error, and that copy must not imply `--diff`/`--since`/CSV/HTML/Markdown-export/reviewer-identity-capture/custom-advisory-override modes exist in V1.8.
decisions.rationale: LAUNCH_PLAN.md is the reference for the launch narrative and asset checklist; Must-publish-at-launch and the Expectation-mismatch-on-MCP risk are read by every channel owner before publishing, so M27 presence there prevents drift in secondary channels. The Challenge-To-Prior-Framing bullet keeps the file's pattern of documenting why launch copy had to be sharpened at each milestone.

decisions.statement: Updated .planning/CONTENT_CALENDAR.md to add: (a) a launch-day terminal-first-post callout naming M27 (V1.8), the new `redaction` CLI noun, the `tusq redaction review [--manifest <path>] [--capability <name>] [--json]` surface, the strictly-read-only-no-manifest-mutation-no-digest-flip invariant, the zero-digest-flip / observationally-invisible-in-`tusq diff` invariant, the `capabilities: []` valid-exit-0-scaffold-state rule with the exact `No capabilities in manifest — nothing to review.` human line, the missing-`manifest_version`/`generated_at` null/unknown-copy-verbatim-no-re-stamp rule, and the Constraints 19/20 reviewer-aid framing boundary — NOT runtime enforcement, NOT automated PII compliance, NOT automated redaction policy, NOT GDPR/HIPAA/PCI-compliant; and (b) a new Day 3-4 'a reviewer-facing PII redaction review report, without touching the manifest' post covering the complete M27 surface: the new `redaction` noun parallel to `policy` with the single `review` subcommand, the frozen nine-entry `PII_REVIEW_ADVISORY_BY_CATEGORY` lookup in `src/cli.js` with em-dash U+2014 byte-exactness locked by a smoke fixture, the reviewer-directive reminder at the end of every advisory, the strictly-read-only invariant with unchanged downstream-command behavior, the zero-digest-flip invariant versus M25's narrow flip and M26's broad flip, the empty-capabilities valid-exit-0 rule distinct from the missing-capabilities-array error path, the null/unknown fallback for missing manifest_version/generated_at with no wall-clock re-stamping, the empty-stdout-on-every-exit-1-path rule with stderr-only error text, the V1.8 flag surface exactly `--manifest`/`--capability`/`--json` (no `--diff`/`--since`/CSV/HTML/Markdown export/custom-advisory-override/locale-selector), and the deliberate reviewer-aid framing under Constraints 19 and 20.
decisions.rationale: CONTENT_CALENDAR.md owns when each launch touchpoint goes out; pushing M27 into launch-day AND a dedicated Day 3-4 post mirrors the M24/M25/M26 pattern, gives the community feed a specific moment to highlight the milestone, and ensures the terminal-first launch-day post doesn't silently skip a shipped CLI noun.

decisions.statement: Updated .planning/ANNOUNCEMENT.md to add: (a) a new step 16 in the product-summary workflow covering the complete M27 surface — `tusq redaction review [--manifest <path>] [--capability <name>] [--json]` as a new `redaction` top-level CLI noun (13th shipped top-level entry point, parallel to `policy`, single `review` subcommand), the per-capability report shape (`approved`/`sensitivity_class`/`pii_fields`/`pii_categories`/deduplicated `advisories`), the distinct-category-first-appearance-order advisories rule with the concrete `['email', 'secrets', 'email']` → two-advisories example, the top-level `manifest_path`/`manifest_version`/`generated_at` copy-verbatim-no-re-stamp rule with null/unknown fallbacks, the strictly-read-only invariant (manifest mtime/content unchanged, no `.tusq/` artifact read or written, `capability_digest` never recomputed, all other commands byte-for-byte identical pre- and post-M27), the zero-digest-flip invariant (observationally invisible in `tusq diff`, in contrast with M25's narrow flip and M26's broad flip), the byte-identical-across-runs invariant asserted by the new `redaction-review-determinism` eval scenario, the `capabilities: []` valid-exit-0-scaffold-state rule distinct from the `Invalid manifest: missing capabilities array` error path, the empty-stdout-on-every-exit-1-path rule with stderr-only error text, the em-dash U+2014 byte-exactness invariant locked by a smoke fixture with reviewer-directive advisory endings and the total-disjoint-frozen-V1.8-set rule, the V1.8 flag surface exactly `--manifest`/`--capability`/`--json` (no `--diff`/`--since`/CSV/HTML/Markdown export/custom-advisory-override/locale-selector), and the Constraints 19/20 reviewer-aid framing boundary with the explicit 'running this command does not make any capability safer at runtime' callout; (b) an updated proof-points paragraph appending the M27 read-only reviewer-facing PII redaction review surface with zero-digest-flip / valid-empty-capabilities / null-unknown-no-re-stamp / empty-stdout-stderr-only / em-dash-byte-exact / reviewer-aid-under-Constraints-19-and-20 invariants; (c) a bash-block comment block inserted after `tusq docs --out docs/capabilities.md` explaining what `tusq redaction review --json` does, the zero-digest-flip/observationally-invisible invariant, the `capabilities: []` exit-0 behavior, the empty-stdout-on-failure rule, the distinct-category-first-appearance-order rule, the em-dash byte-exactness rule, and the reviewer-aid framing; (d) an updated How To Try It Today bullet for `tusq redaction review` with inspection-workflow framing, strictly-read-only callouts, `capabilities: []` valid-exit-0 rule, null/unknown fallback rule, empty-stdout rule, em-dash byte-exactness rule, and explicit NOT-PII-detection / NOT-automated-redaction-policy / NOT-GDPR-HIPAA-PCI-compliance framing; (e) an X-post update naming the M27 report with zero-digest-flip / deterministic / em-dash-byte-exact / valid-empty-capabilities / NOT-runtime-PII-enforcement framing; (f) a Community-post shipped-commands line update appending `redaction review` to the 12-entry subcommand list to reflect the 13-entry shipped surface; and (g) a Community-post V1 scope bullet beneath the existing M25 PII-hint bullet covering the M27 surface with its complete invariant set (new CLI noun parallel to `policy`, flag surface, per-capability report shape, strictly-read-only, zero-digest-flip, determinism, valid-empty-capabilities, null-unknown-no-re-stamp, empty-stdout-stderr-only, em-dash-byte-exact, total-disjoint-frozen-V1.8-set, reviewer-aid framing under Constraints 19/20).
decisions.rationale: ANNOUNCEMENT.md is the longest-form launch artifact with the highest reach — product-summary/proof-points/blog-draft/bash-block/How-To-Try-It-Today/X-post/LinkedIn/community-post all need M27 to land at once or secondary channels will inherit older framing; the community-post shipped-commands list in particular is often quoted verbatim by early adopters, so upgrading it from 12 entries to 13 (including `redaction review`) prevents the most obvious CLI-surface drift claim.

Did NOT modify PM-owned planning artifacts (PM_SIGNOFF.md, ROADMAP.md, SYSTEM_SPEC.md, command-surface.md) because launch phase is not the right time to mutate planning-gate artifacts and the planning_signoff gate for M27 already passed. Did NOT modify QA-owned acceptance-matrix.md / ship-verdict.md / RELEASE_NOTES.md / website/docs/manifest-format.md — M27 implementation, acceptance criteria, release notes, and developer documentation already carry the shipped-state framing on HEAD 93c84b3 and were the source I referenced for canonical M27 claims (especially RELEASE_NOTES.md lines 416-454 and SYSTEM_SPEC.md lines 2558-2769). Only the four launch_ready gate artifacts were modified this turn.
decisions.rationale: Write-authority scope discipline — launch-phase PM role owns only the four launch_ready gate artifacts, and Protocol Rule 3 forbids modifying reserved orchestrator state files. Mutating planning/QA/dev-owned files outside the launch_ready gate would violate role scope even though the write-authority field is 'authoritative.'

summary: Performed idle expansion analysis against VISION.md, ROADMAP.md, SYSTEM_SPEC.md, and current project state (M1–M27 complete); proposed M28 Sensitivity Class Inference as next concrete increment with charter, acceptance_contract, and vision_traceability.

decisions.statement: Challenged the prior accepted turn (turn_b68e40fbb7b5d1f4, role=pm, phase=planning) as a planning-only re-verification pass that cannot satisfy the implementation_complete gate or the idle_expansion acceptance contract. This dev turn operates on intake intent intent_1777034815829_43d8 (category=idle_expansion), which requires analysis of VISION.md, ROADMAP.md, SYSTEM_SPEC.md, and current project state — not a repeat of prior PM planning verification.
decisions.rationale: The mandate for this run is idle expansion: derive the next concrete increment. The PM turn verified M27 gate artifacts were still current, which is correct for its role, but it does not constitute implementation of the idle expansion work product. Dev must independently analyse the vision and propose the next intent.

decisions.statement: All V1 milestones M1–M27 are complete and verified on HEAD 22e4cbe. npm test exits 0 with 'Smoke tests passed' and 'Eval regression harness passed (10 scenarios)'. CLI has 13 top-level commands. No open ROADMAP items remain.
decisions.rationale: Independent re-verification confirms the baseline before any gap analysis. The prior dev implementation records in IMPLEMENTATION_NOTES.md are accurate. No regression found.

decisions.statement: Proposed M28: Sensitivity Class Inference from Static Evidence as the next concrete increment. Every capability in V1.8 has sensitivity_class 'unknown'. The inference rules use already-available signals (pii_categories from M26, side_effect_class from M9, auth_hints from M10) with a bounded frozen ordered rule set. Human-set values are always preserved. Zero new CLI commands, zero new dependencies.
decisions.rationale: This is the highest-impact V2 gap per operator value vs. implementation cost. Operators today cannot distinguish PII-sensitive capabilities from public read-only ones without manually reviewing every capability. SYSTEM_SPEC Constraint 16 explicitly deferred sensitivity inference to 'an explicit V2 sensitivity-inference increment ships with its own constraint entry' — M28 is that increment. VISION traceability: 'Data and schema understanding' (detect sensitive fields), 'Auth and permission mapping' (generate least-privilege hints), 'Governance and review workflows' (keep AI surface changes inspectable), 'Reviewable over opaque' (surface what the engine knows, not hide it).

decisions.statement: Vision is NOT exhausted. All V1 VISION.md headings are served, but multiple V2 headings remain unserved: 'Data and schema understanding' (sensitivity inference, retention defaults), 'Auth and permission mapping' (structured auth_requirements, role extraction), 'Runtime instrumentation' (payload observation, hot-path identification), 'Eval and regression infrastructure' (golden task generation), 'Migration and rollout tooling' (staged rollout, kill switches), 'Skill and agent composition' (multi-step domain agents), 'Competitive transition intelligence' (strategic exposure ranking).
decisions.rationale: The V2 and V3 roadmap sections in VISION.md describe capabilities materially beyond the shipped V1.8 surface. Declaring vision_exhausted would be incorrect and would prematurely close the product roadmap.

Every capability currently has sensitivity_class: 'unknown'; M28 makes the governance surface actionable for operators by surfacing a defensible starting classification derived from existing manifest evidence, directly fulfilling VISION.md 'Data and schema understanding: detect sensitive fields such as PII, payments, secrets, and regulated data' and 'Auth and permission mapping: generate least-privilege hints and approval recommendations'.","inference_rules":["Rule 1 (human wins): If prior manifest has sensitivity_class that is not 'unknown' → preserve unchanged.","Rule 2 (restricted): If pii_categories contains any of ['secrets', 'payment', 'government_id'] → 'restricted'.","Rule 3 (confidential-pii): If pii_categories contains any of ['email', 'phone', 'name', 'address', 'date_of_birth', 'network'] → 'confidential'.","Rule 4 (confidential-destructive): If side_effect_class is 'destructive' or 'financial' → 'confidential'.","Rule 5 (internal): If side_effect_class is 'write' or auth_hints is non-empty → 'internal'.","Rule 6 (public): Otherwise → 'public'."],"acceptance_contract":["tusq manifest auto-infers sensitivity_class for capabilities whose prior manifest value is 'unknown' or for new capabilities, using the frozen ordered rule set enumerated in src/cli.js.","Human-set sensitivity_class values (any value other than 'unknown' present in the prior manifest for that capability) are always preserved on manifest regeneration; inference never overwrites a non-'unknown' value.","Inference output is deterministic: identical manifest inputs produce identical sensitivity_class assignments across repeated runs.","No new CLI command, flag, or npm dependency is added; (h) Express/NestJS/Fastify fixture capabilities produce expected classes.","tusq diff, tusq approve, tusq redaction review, tusq policy verify (default and --strict) behavior is unchanged; sensitivity_class in compiled tools and MCP responses carries the newly-inferred value (no field shape change).","Inference rules are explicitly enumerated in src/cli.js as a frozen ordered constant and documented in SYSTEM_SPEC/ROADMAP/command-surface.md under M28 with a new Constraint entry (Constraint 21) that forbids framing the inferred value as a compliance certification.","website/docs/manifest-format.md updated to document the V2 inference rules, the human-override preservation guarantee, the one-time digest-flip/re-approval implication, and the explicit framing that a non-'unknown' inferred class is a heuristic starting point, not a compliance certification.","One new eval scenario (sensitivity-class-inference-determinism) asserting inference is deterministic across 3 runs on a pii-hint-sample fixture and that all six rule branches produce the expected class; total eval scenarios becomes 11."]}

summary: Produced idle_expansion_result proposing M28 (Sensitivity Class Inference from Static Manifest Evidence) as the next concrete increment, with charter, 11-item acceptance contract, vision traceability citing 7 snapshot headings, and explicit alternative-deferred analysis. No source or planning-gate edits; all V1 M1–M27 milestones remain shipped on HEAD cc4ce8b.

decisions.statement: Produced an idle_expansion_result with kind=new_intake_intent naming M28 — Sensitivity Class Inference from Static Manifest Evidence — as the proposed next increment at priority p1, citing 7 VISION.md snapshot headings (Data and schema understanding; Auth and permission mapping; Governance and review workflows; Reviewable over opaque; The canonical artifact; High-leverage in V2; Roadmap shape: V1, V2, V3).
decisions.rationale: Every V1.8 capability currently emits sensitivity_class: 'unknown' (SYSTEM_SPEC Constraint 16 explicitly deferred inference). The signals needed for inference — pii_categories (M26), side_effect_class (M9), auth_hints (M10) — are already in the manifest. A bounded, transparent, deterministic rule set consumes existing evidence, adds no new CLI command, no new flag, no new npm dependency, and makes the governance surface actionable for operators. Alternatives (diff-aware re-approval, Express/NestJS body extraction, `tusq policy suggest`) are enumerated in the result as deferred with rationale; `tusq policy suggest` is a downstream consumer of M28 inference and is blocked until M28 ships.

decisions.statement: Vision is NOT exhausted. All V1 VISION headings are served by M1–M27, but multiple V2/V3 headings remain unserved: Data and schema understanding (sensitivity inference, retention defaults), Auth and permission mapping (structured auth_requirements, role extraction), Runtime instrumentation (payload observation, hot-path identification), Eval and regression infrastructure (golden task generation), Migration and rollout tooling (staged rollout, kill switches), Skill and agent composition (multi-step domain agents), Competitive transition intelligence (strategic exposure ranking).
decisions.rationale: Independent audit of VISION.md against shipped surface confirms explicit V2/V3 gaps. The charter constraint that a vision_exhausted declaration must cover every snapshot heading is NOT satisfiable here; kind=new_intake_intent is the correct branch.

decisions.statement: Formalized 11-item M28 acceptance contract with frozen ordered rule set (R1 preserve → R2 restricted for secrets/payment/government_id → R3 confidential for email/phone/name/address/date_of_birth/network → R4 confidential for destructive/financial → R5 internal for write or non-empty auth_hints → R6 public), explicit digest-flip callout (first post-M28 manifest regeneration will flip capability_digest for any capability whose inferred class differs from 'unknown', resetting approved=false per M13 semantics), rule-ordering precedence smoke test (AC-7g), and a new Constraint 21 reserving reviewer-aid framing and forbidding compliance-certification language.
decisions.rationale: Three ambiguities in the prior dev turn's M28 sketch are closed here to prevent dev-role friction: (i) rule-ordering precedence was implicit — now explicit with an AC asserting R2 wins over R4 when both apply; (ii) digest-flip consequence was not called out as a one-time expected re-approval event — now documented analogous to M26's broad flip; (iii) preservation semantics under 'missing capability' (new capability) vs 'prior value unknown' were ambiguous — now explicit: both paths invoke inference, only non-'unknown' prior values are preserved.

decisions.statement: Did NOT modify the four PM-owned planning_signoff gate artifacts (.planning/PM_SIGNOFF.md, .planning/ROADMAP.md, .planning/SYSTEM_SPEC.md, .planning/command-surface.md) this turn.
decisions.rationale: Per DEC-900 and prior planning DEC-002, M27 planning is already complete and any further edit to the M27-scoped PM gate artifacts without a new charter would be duplicate churn. M28 is a proposed new intake intent and must pass through its own triage → approval → planning cycle; M28-specific edits to SYSTEM_SPEC § M28 / ROADMAP § M28 / command-surface.md § M28 belong in the M28 planning turn, not this idle-expansion turn. Did NOT touch reserved orchestrator state files, dev-owned src/, QA-owned acceptance-matrix.md/ship-verdict.md/RELEASE_NOTES.md, launch-owned MESSAGING.md/LAUNCH_PLAN.md/CONTENT_CALENDAR.md/ANNOUNCEMENT.md, or .planning/VISION.md (human-owned, read-only per charter constraint).

decisions.statement: Challenged the prior accepted turn (turn_b2194361320f2d0f, role=qa, phase=qa on HEAD 1c9a609) and the intervening launch turn (turn_[latest], role=product_marketing, HEAD cc4ce8b) rather than rubber-stamping: both re-verified the M27 shipped boundary but neither produced an idle_expansion_result for the currently-assigned intake intent (intent_1777046032635_2eab, idle-expansion iteration 1). This PM turn produces that result as required by the intent's acceptance contract.
decisions.rationale: The phase is planning and this intent is an idle_expansion charter; its acceptance contract requires a structured idle_expansion_result naming either a new intake intent or a vision_exhausted classification. Re-verifying M27 or editing M27 gate artifacts does not satisfy that contract. The prior dev turn (turn_c53476b839e7413c, DEC-006–DEC-009) produced a compatible analysis under a sibling intent (intent_1777034815829_43d8) but that analysis is in IMPLEMENTATION_NOTES.md (a dev-owned artifact) rather than in a PM-produced idle_expansion_result artifact for this specific intent.

decisions.statement: Status is needs_human and proposed_next_role is 'human' because acceptance of a new intake intent (M28) and transition of the planning phase toward the next increment both require human approval at the planning_signoff exit gate.
decisions.rationale: An agent PM may not unilaterally accept a newly proposed intake intent as the authoritative next charter, nor may it self-transition across a human-gated boundary. The idle_expansion_result is submitted for human triage; phase_transition_request is null because the current planning gate for M27 is already satisfied on-disk but the next planning cycle must be started from the accepted M28 intent, not by unilateral phase advance. run_completion_request is null because planning is not the terminal phase and the run remains active pending human triage of the M28 proposal.

objections.statement: The prior dev turn (turn_c53476b839e7413c) performed idle-expansion analysis in IMPLEMENTATION_NOTES.md (a dev-owned artifact) rather than emitting a structured idle_expansion_result. That analysis's scope and conclusions are compatible with this PM turn's output, but the location was wrong for cross-role consumption: product_marketing and qa roles do not typically consume IMPLEMENTATION_NOTES.md, and the orchestrator's intake layer cannot mechanically lift a Markdown section into a new intake intent. Future idle-expansion intents should be assigned directly to PM (this turn's role) so the structured result lives in the staging artifact where triage can consume it.

summary: Materialized vision-derived charter sketch for static embeddable-surface plan export (chat/palette/widget/voice) as V2 first-increment candidate; appended re-affirmation paragraphs to all four PM-owned planning_signoff artifacts; preserved V1.10 boundary; baseline re-verified (npm test 16 scenarios, 13-command CLI surface).

decisions.statement: Challenged a naive read of intake intent intent_1777171732846_289f (vision_scan p2, charter '[vision] The Promise: embeddable chat, widget, command-palette, and voice surfaces'). 'Vision goal addressed' does NOT mean ship runtime chat + voice + widgets + Ctrl+K palette in one milestone — that would be a multi-quarter V2 program and would break the local-only / static-only / no-new-runtime-dependency invariants that M25-M29 hold. The PM-defensible response is to charter the smallest first increment that addresses the vision goal: a static, deterministic, manifest-only PLANNER for the four surfaces, with the actual chat/widget/palette/voice generators deferred to four named successor milestones (M-Chat-1, M-Palette-1, M-Widget-1, M-Voice-1).
decisions.rationale: VISION.md lines 27 and 193-224 enumerate the four surfaces as V1 commitments, but shipped reality M1-M29 ships only the manifest/policy/governance machinery — zero embeddable surfaces have any implementation. ROADMAP_NEXT_CANDIDATES.md M30-M44 covers plugins/skills/evals/runtime/rollout/governance but does NOT cover the four embeddable surfaces. This is a real scope gap. The shipped pattern (M27/M28/M29) consistently introduces a reviewer-aid that surfaces what the manifest already implies BEFORE any milestone that mutates downstream artifacts — shipping `tusq surface plan` first lets the project discover (a) which capabilities are actually viable per surface under current sensitivity/auth posture, (b) which brand fields the project doesn't yet have, and (c) capability-domain gaps before any code-generating milestone bakes assumptions into a chat/widget/voice runtime.

decisions.statement: Materialized a 10-item acceptance contract sketch for `tusq surface plan` in .planning/ROADMAP_NEXT_CANDIDATES.md § 'Vision-Derived Charter Candidate: Static Embeddable-Surface Plan Export (V2 First Step)'. Frozen four-value `surface` enum (chat|palette|widget|voice). New top-level noun `surface` with single subcommand `plan`. Four-flag surface (--surface, --manifest, --out, --json). Per-surface plan shape: eligible_capabilities, gated_capabilities with frozen six-value reason-code enum (unapproved/restricted_sensitivity/confidential_sensitivity/destructive_side_effect/auth_scheme_unknown/auth_scheme_oauth_pending_v2), entry_points, redaction_posture copy-forward of M27, auth_posture copy-forward of M29, brand_inputs_required (frozen named list — names only, no values), manifest_version/generated_at copy-forward verbatim. Read-only/zero-digest-flip/byte-identical-MCP-and-compile invariants mirror M27/M28/M29. Empty-capabilities valid-exit-0 with `No capabilities in manifest — nothing to plan.` line; empty-stdout-on-exit-1 rule; determinism locked by new eval scenario `surface-plan-determinism` (16 → ≥17). Proposed Constraint 23 forbids 'generates a chat client / renders a widget / hosts a voice interface / runs a command palette / AI surface generator' framings.
decisions.rationale: The 10-item acceptance contract follows the established M27/M28/M29 reviewer-aid pattern: closed enums, frozen rules, deterministic output, read-only invariant, empty-capabilities valid-exit-0 path, empty-stdout-on-exit-1 path, smoke fixture for byte-exactness, eval scenario for determinism, RELEASE_NOTES governance event on any rule change. The frozen six-value gating-reason enum prevents implementer drift; the frozen brand_inputs_required name-only list prevents the planner from accidentally requiring a brand profile to be authored before any surface generator exists; the four named successor milestones make explicit that V1.x is intentionally the planner-only first step and the runtime generators are V2 work.

decisions.statement: Did NOT bind the embeddable-surface charter into any of the four PM-owned planning_signoff gate artifacts (.planning/PM_SIGNOFF.md, .planning/ROADMAP.md, .planning/SYSTEM_SPEC.md, .planning/command-surface.md). The charter sketch lives only in .planning/ROADMAP_NEXT_CANDIDATES.md (the operator-authored candidate venue). Each of the four PM-owned artifacts received only a Run-Specific Re-Affirmation paragraph naming the new candidate charter — they remain anchored at the V1.10 boundary.
decisions.rationale: Binding a new top-level CLI noun (the first since M27 redaction), a new SYSTEM_SPEC § Surface-Plan section, a new Constraint 23, four named successor milestones, and a new acceptance contract into the four planning_signoff gate artifacts is a material scope decision that requires human review. The candidate-vs-bound distinction is the same one applied for prior idle-expansion intents (e.g., M28 was first proposed as an idle_expansion_result before being bound). Per prior PM precedent (DEC-002 across multiple planning turns) and the assignment's 'do not produce duplicate PM revisions' guidance, the PM-defensible move is to materialize the charter sketch in the candidate venue, name it from the four gate artifacts via re-affirmation, and let the operator/orchestrator decide when to bind it as a frozen ROADMAP entry.

decisions.statement: Did NOT modify reserved orchestrator state files (.agentxchain/state.json, history.jsonl, decision-ledger.jsonl, lock.json). Did NOT modify dev-owned src/ or tests/. Did NOT modify QA-owned acceptance-matrix.md / ship-verdict.md / RELEASE_NOTES.md. Did NOT modify launch-owned MESSAGING.md / LAUNCH_PLAN.md / CONTENT_CALENDAR.md / ANNOUNCEMENT.md / IMPLEMENTATION_NOTES.md. Did NOT modify VISION.md (operator-owned). Modified exactly five planning artifacts: PM_SIGNOFF.md, ROADMAP.md, SYSTEM_SPEC.md, command-surface.md (re-affirmation paragraphs only), and ROADMAP_NEXT_CANDIDATES.md (new charter sketch section appended before existing M30).
decisions.rationale: PM write authority is scoped to the four planning_signoff gate artifacts plus operator-authored candidate-backlog files. Reserved state files are orchestrator-owned. Dev/QA/launch artifacts are owned by their respective phases. VISION.md was just updated by the operator in commit a46d3cb and should remain operator-owned. The minimal-touch policy from prior PM precedent applies.

summary: Materialized vision-derived charter sketch for Static MCP Server Descriptor Export (intent_1777173722739_5899) in ROADMAP_NEXT_CANDIDATES.md; appended Run-Specific Re-Affirmation paragraphs to the four PM-owned planning_signoff artifacts; verified V1.10 baseline holds (16 eval scenarios, 13-command CLI, zero source drift).

decisions.statement: Challenged a naive read of intake intent intent_1777173722739_5899 (vision_scan p2, charter '[vision] The Promise: a self-hostable runtime and MCP server'). The vision goal is partially served at V1.10 — `tusq serve` already provides a describe-only local MCP endpoint with tools/list, tools/call, dry_run_plan, and the OSS is CLI-first / file-first / runs entirely locally. The unserved parts are (1) a static MCP server descriptor as a hashable/diffable/signable self-hostable artifact, (2) generated auth adapters for MCP/marketplace/off-platform use, (3) execution runtime beyond describe-only (same-session proxy, dry-run with confirmation, direct execution of approved-read low-risk capabilities, runtime audit/trace), (4) MCP marketplace packaging. Items (2)/(3)/(4) are V2/V3 multi-quarter scope. PM-defensible response: charter the smallest first increment (a static MCP server descriptor export) and defer the rest to four named successor milestones (M-Runtime-Auth-1, M-Runtime-Trace-1, M-Runtime-Exec-1, M-MCP-Marketplace-1).
decisions.rationale: Following the M27/M28/M29 reviewer-aid pattern: introduce a static, deterministic, manifest-only artifact that surfaces what the manifest already implies before any milestone that mutates downstream artifacts or extends runtime behavior. This protects the local-only / static-only / no-new-runtime-dependency invariants that M25-M29 hold.

decisions.statement: Materialized a 10-item acceptance contract sketch for Static MCP Server Descriptor Export in .planning/ROADMAP_NEXT_CANDIDATES.md § 'Vision-Derived Charter Candidate: MCP Server Static Descriptor Export (V2 Self-Host First Step)'. Frozen schema_version literal `tusq.mcp-descriptor.v1`. Closed three-value runtime_posture boolean block (describe_only_supported: true / execution_supported: false / auth_adapter_generation_supported: false). Closed six-value gated_tools reason-code enum (unapproved, restricted_sensitivity, confidential_sensitivity_pending_review, auth_scheme_unknown_pending_v2, auth_scheme_oauth_pending_v2, destructive_side_effect_pending_v2). Frozen registry_metadata named-list (names only, no values). Per-tool fields copy-forward from M9 (side_effect_class), M27 (redaction_advisories), M28 (sensitivity_class), M29 (auth_requirements with closed seven-value auth_scheme enum and closed five-value evidence_source enum). Read-only / zero-digest-flip / byte-identical-MCP-and-compile invariants mirror M27/M28/M29. Empty-capabilities valid-exit-0 with `No capabilities in manifest — nothing to export.` line. Empty-stdout-on-exit-1 rule. Determinism locked by new eval scenario `mcp-descriptor-determinism` (16 → ≥17). Proposed Constraint 24 forbids 'self-hostable runtime generator', 'AI runtime engine', 'MCP marketplace publisher', 'auth adapter generator', 'MCP execution runtime', 'registry-certified output' framings.
decisions.rationale: Locking the output shape and invariants at the candidate stage lets the operator decide form (A/B/C) and bind to an M-number without re-litigating the descriptor's internal contract. The closed enums and copy-forward semantics ensure the descriptor never invents data the manifest does not already establish.

decisions.statement: PM does NOT pre-commit to a noun-vs-flag form for the new command. The vision_scan re-detected the unchecked ROADMAP boxes literally despite every M28 sub-item having a verifiable code/doc artifact: classifySensitivity at src/cli.js:2732, zero-evidence guard at src/cli.js:2741, sensitivity_class hashed into capability_digest at src/cli.js:469/:472, --sensitivity filter at src/cli.js:803-882 with unrecognized-value exit 1 at :842, tusq docs rendering at :1191, tusq diff propagation at :1416, SYSTEM_SPEC § M28 at line 2782, Constraint 21 at line 3218, website/docs/manifest-format.md § 'sensitivity_class rules' at lines 215-246, eval scenarios sensitivity-class-r1-preserve-precedence / sensitivity-class-r4-financial-inference / sensitivity-class-zero-evidence-unknown.
decisions.rationale: Re-implementing an already-shipped milestone would re-run computeCapabilityDigest over identical content and produce no semantic change, but if dispatched against a sloppy diff would risk spurious capability_digest flips and a forced re-approval sweep without a corresponding governance event — violating the spirit of Constraint 18 (no auto-escalation) and Constraint 21 (M28 reviewer-aid framing invariant). PM-defensible response is to reconcile documentation with shipped reality, not re-execute shipped work.

decisions.statement: Flipped all 15 M28 sub-item checkboxes in .planning/ROADMAP.md from `[ ]` to `[x]` and prepended a § M28 reconciliation note enumerating the file:line evidence for every shipped sub-item. decisions.rationale: Each sub-item was independently verified against HEAD 5e1feae before its checkbox was flipped. Leaving stale `[ ]` boxes adjacent to a shipped milestone creates a recurring vision_scan trigger surface and false-positive intake events; reconciling with reality is cheap, deterministic, and prevents future stale charters from being machine-generated against the same artifact.

summary: Bound M30: Static Embeddable-Surface Plan Export from Manifest Evidence (~0.75 day) into ROADMAP.md as the next bounded V1.11 increment derived from VISION.md 'The Promise' embeddable surfaces; added 5 Key Risk rows; recorded charter binding in PM_SIGNOFF.md preamble.

decisions.statement: Challenged prior turn (turn_0f67c476b8fd6477, role=qa, phase=qa, HEAD 307bdfd) explicitly rather than rubber-stamping. The QA turn correctly added REQ-125-128 to acceptance-matrix.md (124 -> 128), updated ship-verdict.md and RELEASE_NOTES.md, ran npm test exit 0 (20 scenarios), and proposed launch transition. That work is upheld and unrelated to the planning-phase charter this turn binds. The intake intent for THIS turn (intent_1777189729185_7034, vision_scan, roadmap_exhausted_vision_open) explicitly required 'concrete unchecked M30 items' — six prior PM/dev recovery turns deferred this binding to human approval, but the auto_approve policy plus the explicit 'Produce concrete unchecked M30 items' instruction makes deferral the wrong call this turn.
decisions.rationale: The orchestrator has re-injected the roadmap-replenishment charter across multiple runs without progress; the prior PM/dev recovery loop produced zero binding. This turn breaks the loop by binding M30 from the most-developed candidate sketch (Static Embeddable-Surface Plan Export), resolving the open scope decision (new top-level noun vs sub-noun) in favor of `tusq surface plan` per M27 `redaction review` precedent.

decisions.statement: Bound M30 as 'Static Embeddable-Surface Plan Export from Manifest Evidence (~0.75 day) — V1.11 (PROPOSED)' in .planning/ROADMAP.md. The milestone introduces a new top-level CLI noun `surface` with single subcommand `plan` (CLI surface 13 -> 14 commands), a frozen four-value `surface` enum (chat | palette | widget | voice), a closed six-value `gated_reason` enum, per-surface eligibility precedence rules, deterministic JSON/human plan output with M27 advisory copy-forward and M29 auth_requirements copy-forward, brand_inputs_required as a frozen names-only list (no values, no brand profile read), read-only invariants mirroring M27/M28/M29, empty-capabilities exit 0 with the 'No capabilities in manifest — nothing to plan.' line, stdout-empty-on-error discipline, eval regression scenario `surface-plan-determinism` (20 -> >=21 scenarios), and proposed Constraint 23 codifying the planning-aid (not surface-generator) framing.
decisions.rationale: This is the smallest first increment that addresses VISION.md 'The Promise' line 27 and the embeddable-surfaces sections (lines 193-224) without committing the project to runtime chat/voice/widget/palette code generation in V1.11. It mirrors the established M27/M28/M29 reviewer-aid pattern: static, deterministic, manifest-only, read-only, no new dependency. The four successor milestones (M-Chat-1, M-Palette-1, M-Widget-1, M-Voice-1) for actual surface generators remain deferred under their own future ROADMAP entries.

decisions.statement: Resolved the open scope decision from the candidate sketch in favor of a new top-level noun `tusq surface plan` (Form A) over flag-only extension or sub-noun under a future planning hub (Forms B and C). Following M27 `redaction review` precedent, M30 introduces `surface` as the 12th alphabetical noun in the CLI surface, growing it from 13 to 14 commands. A future planning hub may subsume `surface plan` later under its own ROADMAP milestone if and when that hub is chartered.
decisions.rationale: Form A is consistent with the shipped pattern (M27 introduced `redaction` as a new noun for `tusq redaction review`); a flag-only form would couple surface planning to an existing command's lifecycle, breaking the bounded-scope invariant; deferring to a hypothetical future planning hub would block M30 indefinitely on a hub-naming bikeshed that the project has not chartered.

decisions.statement: Modified exactly two PM-owned planning artifacts: .planning/ROADMAP.md (M30 milestone block with 17 unchecked items + 5 new Key Risk rows) and .planning/PM_SIGNOFF.md (M30 charter-bound preamble). Did NOT modify .planning/SYSTEM_SPEC.md or .planning/command-surface.md this turn — per the M27/M28/M29 precedent, the SYSTEM_SPEC § M30 section and command-surface § M30 entry are materialized by the dev role during the implementation phase (PM charters scope; dev materializes verbatim shape/algorithm/exit-code details before any code lands). Did NOT modify reserved orchestrator state files (.agentxchain/state.json, history.jsonl, decision-ledger.jsonl, lock.json). Did NOT modify dev-owned src/, bin/, tests/, website/, package.json, package-lock.json, or IMPLEMENTATION_NOTES.md. Did NOT modify QA-owned acceptance-matrix.md / ship-verdict.md / RELEASE_NOTES.md. Did NOT modify launch-owned MESSAGING.md / LAUNCH_PLAN.md / CONTENT_CALENDAR.md / ANNOUNCEMENT.md. Did NOT modify VISION.md (operator-owned) or ROADMAP_NEXT_CANDIDATES.md (the surface-plan candidate is now bound; the MCP-descriptor candidate remains in the file pending its own form-decision turn).
decisions.rationale: The four PM-owned planning_signoff gate artifacts are PM_SIGNOFF.md, ROADMAP.md, SYSTEM_SPEC.md, command-surface.md. The charter's acceptance contract item 1 ('New unchecked milestone items added to .planning/ROADMAP.md') is satisfied by the ROADMAP.md edit; item 2 (scope derived from listed VISION.md sections) is satisfied by VISION.md line 27 + 193-224 grounding; item 3 (bounded, testable, non-duplicate) is satisfied by the 17-item acceptance contract, the eval scenario, and the explicit no-overlap with M1-M29 (all of which are governance/compiler-side, while M30 is the first user-surface-side milestone).

decisions.statement: Setting status=completed and phase_transition_request='implementation' under the run's auto_approve policy. proposed_next_role='dev'. The planning_signoff gate's required artifacts (PM_SIGNOFF.md, ROADMAP.md, SYSTEM_SPEC.md) all exist; M30 is bound in ROADMAP.md and announced in PM_SIGNOFF.md. SYSTEM_SPEC.md is not modified this turn but exists with V1.10 content and the M30 § will be materialized in the implementation phase per established precedent. The mandate explicitly states 'Do NOT set status: needs_human solely to request phase-gate approval' when auto_approve is configured.
decisions.rationale: M30 is implementation-ready planned work. The dev role owns shipping the 17 acceptance items, materializing SYSTEM_SPEC § M30 with the verbatim shape/algorithm/eligibility-precedence details, adding the `surface-plan-determinism` eval scenario, and updating website docs. QA role owns REQ-129+ verification.

objections.statement: The MCP-descriptor candidate (.planning/ROADMAP_NEXT_CANDIDATES.md § 'MCP Server Static Descriptor Export (V2 Self-Host First Step)') remains unbound after this turn. Its form-decision A/B/C blocker (new top-level noun `mcp` with subcommand `export` vs flag-only extension `tusq serve --export <path>` vs subcommand under a future `plan` hub `tusq plan mcp`) is unresolved. Reserved for a future PM turn after M30 ships. Not a blocker for M30; recorded so future runs do not re-derive the candidate from VISION.md without checking this objection.
